title: Biophytum myriophyllum
taxonomy: Biophytum myriophyllum is a species of flowering plant in the family Oxalidaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and is also known as the 'air potato'. The plant is a climber with long stems and leaves that resemble a fern. Biophytum myriophyllum is classified in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Oxalidales, and family Oxalidaceae. It is a popular ornamental plant due to its unique and attractive foliage.
uses: Biophytum myriophyllum is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. The plant is said to be useful in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory diseases. Its leaves and roots are used in traditional medicine, and it is also used as a digestive aid and to treat various skin conditions. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Biophytum myriophyllum for medicinal purposes.

intro: Biophytum myriophyllum, also known as "Konnar" or "Chaulmoogra", is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the family Oxalidaceae, characterized by its sprawling habit and numerous leaves. The health benefits of Biophytum myriophyllum include its potential in treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory disorders, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Biophytum myriophyllum is used to prepare various herbal formulations, such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, often in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
